# @byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-consul/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-consul/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch-protocol-consul/CHANGELOG.md)

> The following is **@byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul@2.x** document, **1.x** document see [fetch-v1.x](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/fetch-v1.x/packages/fetch-protocol-consul), [@byted-service/fetch@2.0.0 release log](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnMNUcH3YbXKEeZSX4YqCavc).

`@byted-service/fetch` consul protocol extention.

1. support `consul://p.s.m/xxx`, [Node.js Service Discovery (Consul) User Guide](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnU8nNhyPfhV2OjHLCVQPX5g), [A Quick Start with Service Discovery ( Consul ) for Front-End Engineers](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn9QfY7pMwo9I9Jf1Y2q6R4d).
2. suport [ByteMesh](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn3Kazqb9H1MZsHBet1q6RGc), [ByteMesh HTTP/1.1 & HTTP/2 protocol access agreement](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnBvELWkcTSb7PMU3H0GoRqd).

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Quick Start

> Currently it is built-in in @byted-service/fetch, actually can be used without any operation.

```ts
import { fetch } from '@byted-service/fetch';
import { protocol, handle } from '@byted-service/fetch-protocol-consul';

fetch.register(protocol, handle);

(async () => {
    const resp = await fetch('consul://p.s.m/xxx');

    console.log(await resp.text());
})();
```

## API

### fetch(url[, options])

The parameters of `@byted-service/fetch` have been extended. Generally, It is set by framework , and user unaware.

<details>
  <summary>History</summary>

-   `v2.1.0`
    -   Mark `options.consulClient`, `options.consulClientOptions`, `options.disableMesh` as deprecated.
    -   Support `options.consulOption`, `options.consulLookupOption`, `options.enableMesh`.

</details>

-   `url`: request url.
-   `options`:
    -   `consul`: `Consul` instance, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
    -   `consulOptions`: `Consul` construction options, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
    -   `consulLookupOptions`: `consul.lookup()` options, see [@byted-service/consul](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md).
    -   `enableMesh`: Whether to send request through `http egress mesh`, the default value is `true`.

## Mesh

Starting from `@byted-service/fetch@1.6.0`, when the `tce` cluster is configured according to https://bytedance.feishu.cn/space/doc/doccnjPUcpCENG6vktQYID, requests in the form of `consul:` will be sent through the mesh proxy.

If it is in `faas && enable mesh and SERVICE_MESH_HTTP_EGRESS_ADDR is existed`, consul protocol will use [@byted/node-fetch](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-node-fetch) to initiate a request.

## Metrics

Related indicators and tags are the same as [@byted-service/fetch](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch/README.en.md#metrics), the modified part is listed below.

-   `to`: Server psm.
-   `to_cluster`: Server cluster, can be set by `options.consul.cluster`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `to_idc`: Server idc, can be set by `options.consul.idc`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `mesh`: Whether use http egress mesh, the default is `0`, judge according to environment variables and `options.disableMesh`.
    -   `0`: No, `options.disableMesh = false` or `process.env.TCE_ENABLE_HTTP_SIDECAR_EGRESS !=='True'`, at this time, the address after consul service discovery will be requested.
    -   `1`: Yes, `options.disableMesh = true` or `process.env.TCE_ENABLE_HTTP_SIDECAR_EGRESS ==='True'`, at this time, if on faas, `process.env.SERVICE_MESH_HTTP_EGRESS_ADDR` will be requested, otherwise, `127.0.0.1:process. env.MESH_EGRESS_PORT`.
-   `ipv6`: The consul service found whether the returned ipv6 address, the default is `0`.
    -   `0`: No.
    -   `1`: Yes.

## Logger

Related tags are the same as [@byted-service/fetch](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/fetch/README.en.md#logger), the modified part is listed below.

<details>
  <summary>History</summary>

-   `v2.1.0`: Remove `is_fass_http_egress`.

</details>

-   `to`: Server psm.
-   `to_cluster`: Server cluster, can be set by `options.consul.cluster`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `to_idc`: Server idc, can be set by `options.consul.idc`, the result of consul service discovery may be changed after modification.
-   `mesh`: Whether use http egress mesh, the default is `0`, judge according to environment variables and `options.disableMesh`.
    -   `0`: No, `options.disableMesh = false` or `process.env.TCE_ENABLE_HTTP_SIDECAR_EGRESS !=='True'`, at this time, the address after consul service discovery will be requested.
    -   `1`: Yes, `options.disableMesh = true` or `process.env.TCE_ENABLE_HTTP_SIDECAR_EGRESS ==='True'`, at this time, if on faas, `process.env.SERVICE_MESH_HTTP_EGRESS_ADDR` will be requested, otherwise, `127.0.0.1:process. env.MESH_EGRESS_PORT`.
-   `rip`: Real request ip.
-   `ipv6`: The consul service found whether the returned ipv6 address, the default is `0`.
    -   `0`: No.
    -   `1`: Yes.
